title: The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths
content:
{{Top|ga|leg|italics=1}} {{InUniverseMedia |image= |name=''The Brief Reign of
Future Wraiths'' |author= |editor= |illustrator= |artist= |performer=[[Unidentified Bith opera company|Bith opera company]]''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' |publisher= |release date= |media type=[[Opera/Legends|Opera]] |pages= |published in= |genre= }} {{Quote|While I'd be glad to discuss these matters at greater length, the performance is about to begin.|Palpatine, ending a discussion with fellow senators prior to a performance of ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths''|}} '''''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths''''' was a popular [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Opera/Legends|opera]] underwritten by members of [[House Valorum/Legends|House Valorum]]. A [[Unidentified Bith opera company|company]] of [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] had toured the opera across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] by [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. In that year, the company staged ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' at the [[Coruscant Opera/Legends|Coruscant Opera]], which was attended by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] and many other [[Senator/Legends|senators]]. ==Description== ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' was an [[Opera/Legends|opera]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] that was [[Currency/Legends|funded]] by two [[Finis Valorum's father|members]] of the influential [[House Valorum/Legends|House Valorum]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], in addition to a number of other notable works they had supported.{{FactFile|56|VAL 1-4|Finis Valorum}} ==History== [[File:Coruscant-EotE.jpg|thumb|right|250px|''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' made a long-awaited return to Coruscant (pictured) in 32 BBY.]] ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' was popular{{FactFile|109|LEI 9-10|Leisure}} and critically acclaimed, serving as a mainstay at the [[Mirrsteel Grande Opera House]] on the [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]].''[[Lords of Nal Hutta]]'' In [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]],''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' states that ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' had been performed by the [[Unidentified Bith opera company|Bith opera company]] for twenty [[Standard year/Legends|years]], which, given ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''Cloak of Deception'' in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], places the beginning of the company's tour in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]]. a [[Unidentified Bith opera company|company]] of [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] began performing the opera in venues of the operatic world across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' had a long-awaited return to Coruscant, where it was performed at the [[Coruscant Opera/Legends|Coruscant Opera]]. The [[Reproduction/Legends|son]] of the opera's financiers, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], was in attendance for the returning performance, driving up ticket prices and making them as difficult to obtain as [[Adegan crystal/Legends|Adegan crystals]], a rare type of [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]]. Keeping with customs, Valorum arrived late to the performance, receiving standing applause from the audience while he and his [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Sei Taria/Legends|Sei Taria]], entered. Before taking a seat in his family's reserved balcony, Valorum nodded to a private balcony opposite his, which contained Republic [[Senator/Legends|senators]] [[Orn Free Taa/Legends|Orn Free Taa]], [[Toonbuck Toora/Legends|Toonbuck Toora]], [[Passel Argente/Legends|Passel Argente]], [[Edcel Bar Gane/Legends|Edcel Bar Gane]], and [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], along with the latter's aides [[Kinman Doriana/Legends|Kinman Doriana]] and [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] and Taa's [[Orn Free Taa's Twi'lek aide|consort]]. Also in attendance throughout the building were Senators [[Bail Antilles/Legends|Bail Antilles]], [[Horox Ryyder/Legends|Horox Ryyder]], and [[Tendau Bendon/Legends|Tendau Bendon]]. Prior to the beginning of the performance, Palpatine and the senators in his balcony discussed recent developments about Valorum's budding [[Senate Resolution BR-0371|tax plan]]. Palpatine rejected Toora's request for more questions about his own [[Politics/Legends|political]] ideals as the beginning of the performance drew near. ==Behind the scenes== ''The Brief Reign of Future Wraiths'' was first mentioned in [[James Luceno]]'s [[2001]] novel ''[[Cloak of Deception]]''. The [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 56|fifty-sixth issue]] of the [[De Agostini]] magazine ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]'' written by [[Iain Lowson]]{{LinkedIn|url=in/iainlowson|text=Iain Lowson|archivefile=File:IainLowson-LinkedIn.png}} and released around [[January 22]], [[2003]],{{FFdates|56}} referred to the title of the opera as an ironic one in that it alluded to Palpatine[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] as a "wraith" who ultimately engineered Valorum's downfall. ==Appearances== *''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' {{1stm}} ==Sources== *{{FactFile|56|VAL 1-4|Finis Valorum}} *{{FactFile|109|LEI 9-10|Leisure}} *''[[Lords of Nal Hutta]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Die kurze Herrschaft der Zukunftsgeister }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Brief Reign of Future Wraiths, The}} [[Category:Corellian culture]] [[Category:Operas]]
